output:
## Loan Balance Calculation

Working through each month with the process: **apply 2% interest → check late fee → subtract $150 payment**.

| Month | Start Balance | After Interest (×1.02) | Late Fee | After Fee | After $150 Payment |
|-------|--------------|----------------------|----------|-----------|-------------------|
| 1 | $5,000.00 | $5,100.00 | $75 | $5,175.00 | **$5,025.00** |
| 2 | $5,025.00 | $5,125.50 | $75 | $5,200.50 | **$5,050.50** |
| 3 | $5,050.50 | $5,151.51 | $75 | $5,226.51 | **$5,076.51** |
| 4 | $5,076.51 | $5,178.04 | $75 | $5,253.04 | **$5,103.04** |
| 5 | $5,103.04 | $5,205.10 | $75 | $5,280.10 | **$5,130.10** |
| 6 | $5,130.10 | $5,232.70 | $75 | $5,307.70 | **$5,157.70** |

The balance after interest exceeds $5,000 every single month, so the $75 late fee applies all 6 months. The $150 payment isn't enough to overcome the ~$100 monthly interest plus $75 fee, so the balance grows each month.

**After 6 months, the loan balance is $5,158.**
